qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Bazaar plugin uninstall path traversal permits arbitrary workspace deletion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets `POST /api/bazaar/uninstallBazaarPlugin` deleting attacker-chosen paths via `os.RemoveAll`, while the advisory targets `POST /api/file/globalCopyFiles` copying attacker-supplied source files into the workspace because source paths are not confined to the workspace. Same repository, but different endpoint, sink, and root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable `globalCopyFiles` file-copy logic or its missing source-path confinement. The only qualifying finding is a separate bazaar uninstall traversal that reaches `os.RemoveAll` and causes deletion, not arbitrary file read.
evidence: The advisory describes `POST /api/file/globalCopyFiles` in `kernel/api/file.go`, where attacker-controlled `srcs` are copied into the workspace without confinement to authorized workspace paths.

The only final `qualifying_vuln` finding instead describes `POST /api/bazaar/uninstallBazaarPlugin` in `kernel/api/bazaar.go` forwarding attacker-controlled `packageName` into `model.UninstallBazaarPlugin` and ultimately `os.RemoveAll`.
The finding's PoC report demonstrates deletion of `temp/victim-delete-me`; it does not demonstrate copying external files into the workspace or reading them back through the file retrieval API.
